questions.objective_ref: Define acceptable error as a deliberate threshold decision — not zero errors, but a bounded tolerance for specific types of failure within a defined use case.
Distinguish false positives (Type I errors) from false negatives (Type II errors) and explain why their costs typically differ.
Explain why the same error rate can be acceptable in one context and unacceptable in another, using at least two contrasting examples.
Apply a four-step process to define a failure threshold for a given AI use case.
Describe the human baseline and explain why it is a useful but insufficient reference point for setting AI error thresholds.
Recognise alert fatigue as the direct consequence of acceptable error thresholds set too strictly.
